name: browshot.com
prose:
# Browshot API API

Take screenshots of any website in real time

## Getting started

The Browshot API API is a JSON REST API. All endpoints are relative to the base URL `https://api.browshot.com/api/v1`.

## Authentication

Authenticate by passing your API key in the `key` header on every request.

## API reference

### GET /account/info
Get information about your account
Returns a single record.

### POST /batch/ceate
Requests thousands of screenshtos at once

### GET /batch/info
Get the batch status
Returns a single record.

### GET /browser/info
Get information about a browser
Returns a single record.

### GET /browser/list
Get all browsers
Returns a single record.

### GET /instance/info
Get information about an instance
Returns a single record.

### GET /instance/list
Get all instances
Returns a single record.

### GET /screenshot/create
Request a screenshot
Returns a single record.

### GET /screenshot/delete
Delete screenshot data
Returns a list of records.
Accepts query parameters: `id`, `data`.

Sample response:

```json
[ { "id": 1 }, { "id": 2 } ]
```

### GET /screenshot/host
Host thumbnails on your own S3 account or on Browshot.
Returns a list of records.
Accepts query parameters: `id`, `hosting`, `width`, `height`, `scale`, `bucket`, `file`, `headers`.

Sample response:

```json
[ { "id": 1 }, { "id": 2 } ]
```

### GET /screenshot/html
Get the HTML code
Returns a single record.

### GET /screenshot/info
Query screenshot status
Returns a list of records.
Accepts query parameters: `id`, `details`.

Sample response:

```json
[ { "id": 1 }, { "id": 2 } ]
```

### GET /screenshot/list
Get information about screenshots
Returns a list of records.
Accepts query parameters: `limit`, `status`.

Sample response:

```json
[ { "id": 1 }, { "id": 2 } ]
```

### GET /screenshot/multiple
Request multiple screenshots
Returns a single record.

### GET /screenshot/search
Search for screenshots
Returns a list of records.
Accepts query parameters: `url`, `limit`, `status`.

Sample response:

```json
[ { "id": 1 }, { "id": 2 } ]
```

### GET /screenshot/share
Share a screenshot
Returns a list of records.
Accepts query parameters: `id`, `note`.

Sample response:

```json
[ { "id": 1 }, { "id": 2 } ]
```

### GET /screenshot/thumbnail
Retrieve a thumbnail image
Returns a single record.
